_source: Vehicle curve: widely-cited new-car depreciation studies (iSeeCars.com annual 5-Year Depreciation study; Carfax/Consumer Reports commentary) commonly summarized as 'a new car loses ~20% of its value in year one and ~60% of its value by year five', tapering afterward. The fractions below (fraction of original purchase price retained, indexed by completed years of age) approximate that commonly-cited curve out to 10 years, flat beyond that. Appliance/system model: straight-line depreciation to a small salvage value is the standard 'Actual Cash Value' (ACV) method used industry-wide for insurance claims on household personal property (Insurance Information Institute; see also IRS Publication 946 for the general concept of spreading an asset's cost over its useful life) — value declines linearly from purchase price to a salvage floor over the item's expected lifespan.

vehicle.note: Index = completed years of age (0 = brand new). Age beyond the table length uses the last value.

appliance.note: value = purchasePrice * max(salvageFraction, 1 - ageYears / lifespanYears); lifespanYears comes from lifespans.json for the matched subtype.
